_meta.description: Hand-curated K-Beauty fixture → key ingredient mapping for MFDS regulatory anchor. Narrow scope: sunscreen UV filters only, where MFDS data has dramatic country-specific regulation differences. Skincare actives (Centella, snail mucin, niacinamide) are safe per MFDS and skipped here.
_meta.reviewer: MFDS coverage analysis showed <30% fixture-level benefit for general K-Beauty; narrowing to sunscreen category only avoids noise injection on other fixtures (per Phase F.3 rejection criteria in PHASE_F3_MFDS_DESIGN.md).

boj-relief-sun.productKo: Beauty of Joseon Relief Sun: Rice + Probiotics SPF50+
boj-relief-sun.notes: Korean-style chemical sunscreen — no inorganic (TiO2/ZnO) — uses next-gen UV filters approved in EU/KR/ASEAN/JP but several are NOT approved in the US (FDA still uses 1999 monograph). This is the precise mismatch the MFDS anchor surfaces: ingredient legal in KR/EU, restricted/unapproved in US/AU.
